title: Molecular Mechanisms of Psilocybin and Implications for the Treatment of Depression.

assets.abstract: Therapeutic deficiencies with monoaminergic antidepressants invites the need to identify and develop novel rapid-acting antidepressants. Hitherto, ketamine and esketamine are identified as safe, well-tolerated rapid-acting antidepressants in adults with treatment-resistant depression, and also mitigate measures of suicidality. Psilocybin is a naturally occurring psychoactive alkaloid and non-selective agonist at many serotonin receptors, especially at serotonin 5-HT2A receptors, and is found in

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Psychedelics — DMN disruption, ego dissolution, altered states, predictive processing decoupling.
